items.title: Moonshot AI Releases Kimi K2.6 with Long-Horizon Coding, Agent Swarm Scaling to 300 Sub-Agents and 4,000 Coordinated Steps
items.summary:
Moonshot AI, the Chinese AI lab behind the Kimi assistant, today open-sourced Kimi K2.6 — a native multimodal agentic model that pushes the boundaries of what an AI system can do when left to run autonomously on hard software engineering problems. The release targets practical deployment scenarios: long-running coding agents, front-end generation from natural language, […]
The post Moonshot AI Releases Kimi K2.6 with Long-Horizon Coding, Agent Swarm Scaling to 300 Sub-Agents and 4,000 Coordinated Steps appeared first on MarkTechPost.

items.title: A Coding Implementation on Microsoft’s Phi-4-Mini for Quantized Inference Reasoning Tool Use RAG and LoRA Fine-Tuning
items.summary:
In this tutorial, we build a pipeline on Phi-4-mini to explore how a compact yet highly capable language model can handle a full range of modern LLM workflows within a single notebook. We begin by setting up a stable environment, loading Microsoft’s Phi-4-mini-instruct in efficient 4-bit quantization, and then move step by step through streaming […]
The post A Coding Implementation on Microsoft’s Phi-4-Mini for Quantized Inference Reasoning Tool Use RAG and LoRA Fine-Tuning appeared first on MarkTechPost.

items.title: Google’s AI Overviews Produce Hundreds of Millions of Inaccurate Answers Every Day, Analysis Suggests
items.summary:
A new Oumi analysis reveals Google's AI Overviews may produce up to 225 billion false summaries annually. Learn about the accuracy gap between Gemini 2 and Gemini 3.
The post Google’s AI Overviews Produce Hundreds of Millions of Inaccurate Answers Every Day, Analysis Suggests appeared first on TechRepublic.

items.title: Scaling Codex to enterprises worldwide
items.summary: OpenAI launches Codex Labs, partners with with Accenture, PwC, Infosys, and others to help enterprises deploy and scale Codex across the software development lifecycle, and hits 4M Codex WAU.

items.title: European Commission Moving to Classify ChatGPT as ‘Very Large Online Search Engine’ Under Digital Services Act
items.summary:
The European Commission is set to designate ChatGPT as a ‘Very Large Online Search Engine,’ subjecting OpenAI to strict Digital Services Act compliance rules.
The post European Commission Moving to Classify ChatGPT as ‘Very Large Online Search Engine’ Under Digital Services Act appeared first on TechRepublic.

items.title: OpenAI recruits Cognizant and CGI to take Codex into enterprise software shops worldwide
items.summary:
OpenAI is building a systems integrator channel for Codex, enlisting large consulting firms to carry the coding agent into organisations it cannot reach through direct sales. Cognizant and CGI are the first named SI partners in the programme, announced on the same day. Codex has grown 6x among ChatGPT Business and Enterprise users since January. […]
This story continues at The Next Web

items.title: Amazon Deepens Anthropic Partnership, Eyeing Up to $25B as Claude Demand Surges
items.summary:
Amazon is investing another $5 billion in Anthropic, deepening its AWS partnership as Claude expands across chips, cloud, and enterprise access.
The post Amazon Deepens Anthropic Partnership, Eyeing Up to $25B as Claude Demand Surges appeared first on TechRepublic.

items.title: A Coding Implementation on Qwen 3.6-35B-A3B Covering Multimodal Inference, Thinking Control, Tool Calling, MoE Routing, RAG, and Session Persistence
items.summary:
In this tutorial, we build an end-to-end implementation around Qwen 3.6-35B-A3B and explore how a modern multimodal MoE model can be used in practical workflows. We begin by setting up the environment, loading the model adaptively based on available GPU memory, and creating a reusable chat framework that supports both standard responses and explicit thinking […]
The post A Coding Implementation on Qwen 3.6-35B-A3B Covering Multimodal Inference, Thinking Control, Tool Calling, MoE Routing, RAG, and Session Persistence appeared first on MarkTechPost.

items.title: Amazon puts up to $25 billion more into Anthropic and secures 10-year cloud commitment in return
items.summary:
The deal is Amazon’s second consecutive mega-round with a frontier AI lab, following a near-identical $50 billion investment in OpenAI two months ago. Anthropic’s annualised revenue has reached $30 billion. The arrangement locks Anthropic into AWS’s Trainium chips through Trainium4 and secures up to 5 gigawatts of compute capacity for Claude. Amazon has agreed to […]
This story continues at The Next Web

items.title: Ordering with the Starbucks ChatGPT app was a true coffee nightmare
items.summary: Venti iced coffee, light skim milk. That's what I get at Starbucks. It is what I have gotten at Starbucks every time I've been to Starbucks for as long as I can remember, other than a brief love affair with the caffe misto a few years ago. In person, my brain barely needs to activate […]

items.title: GitHub freezes new Copilot sign-ups as agentic AI breaks the economics of flat-rate developer subscriptions
items.summary:
Agentic coding workflows are now routinely generating costs that exceed what users pay per month. GitHub’s response, pausing new sign-ups for Pro, Pro+, and Student plans and tightening usage caps, signals that the era of unlimited AI assistance at fixed prices is ending. GitHub has paused new sign-ups for its Copilot Pro, Pro+, and Student […]
This story continues at The Next Web

items.title: Trump says Anthropic Pentagon deal is ‘possible’, weeks after blacklisting the company as a national security risk
items.summary:
The US president told CNBC on Tuesday that Anthropic is ‘shaping up’ following a White House meeting last Friday at which the company’s CEO Dario Amodei discussed its Mythos AI model with Chief of Staff Susie Wiles and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent. The Pentagon’s blacklisting of Anthropic remains in legal limbo, with a federal appeals […]
This story continues at The Next Web
